items.q: What does gravitational field strength at a point represent?
items.correct: The force per unit mass that would act on a test mass placed at that point.
items.misconception: The total gravitational force at that point regardless of mass.
items.random_wrong: The mass of the object creating the field.
items.why: Students forget the 'per unit mass' (or per unit charge) part of field strength definitions, conflating the field with the resulting force.

items.q: In a balanced nuclear equation, what must be equal on both sides?
items.correct: The total mass number (top numbers) and the total atomic number (bottom numbers) must both balance.
items.misconception: Only the atomic number (proton count) needs to balance.
items.random_wrong: Only the total mass in kilograms needs to balance.
items.why: Students transfer 'balance the atoms' from chemical equations, but in nuclear equations identities can change while nucleon and charge counts are conserved.

items.q: What determines the energy of a single photon?
items.correct: Its frequency (E = hf) — only frequency, not intensity.
items.misconception: A brighter light has higher-energy photons because intensity makes each photon more energetic.
items.random_wrong: Photon energy depends on how fast the photon is moving relative to the source.
items.why: Students conflate intensity (number of photons) with photon energy (frequency-dependent); this is why dim UV is more dangerous than bright red.

items.q: Redshift of distant galaxies is evidence that:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: the universe is expanding. “the universe is contracting” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: The Sun is currently in which stage?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Main sequence. “Protostar” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If the universe is 13.8 billion years old, how many seconds is that? (1 year ≈ 3.15×10⁷ s; answer in 10¹⁷ s)
items.why: The real figure is 4.35 x10^17 s. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
